dialogueChunks.intro: You're back! But... your face tells me something went wrong out there.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} have set up a camp right along the path we need to {quest_action}. They weren't there before, I swear it. {target_npc} says they've been moving closer all week. You'll need to clear them out or find another way through to {quest_focus}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm so sorry to pile more trouble on you. {quest_stakes} are counting on you more than ever now.

playerResponses.text: A few monsters won't stop me. I'll push through.
This just got harder. I'll need something extra for my trouble.
This is getting too dangerous. I'm done.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I... I don't know how to say this. Something terrible has happened while you were away.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} took what little {quest_item} we had stockpiled and fled toward {location_hint}. Said they couldn't wait any longer and were going to trade it for safe passage out of the region. Without those supplies, our efforts to {quest_action} are set back completely. We need you to track them down and recover what they took.
dialogueChunks.outro: I trusted them. We all did. Please, bring back what's ours before {quest_stakes} suffer even more.

playerResponses.text: I'll find them. They couldn't have gotten far.
Chasing down your own people now? This keeps getting worse.
Sort out your own problems. I've done enough.

dialogueChunks.intro: Oh no. No, no, no. {player_name}, we have a new problem.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {quest_item} you found? It's not enough. {quest_threat} has spread faster than we thought, and now we need three times the amount to {quest_action}. Worse, the only place to find more is deep in {enemy_type} territory {location_hint}. I've heard there's a cave system where it grows thick on the walls, but nobody who's gone in has come back out.
dialogueChunks.outro: I would never ask this if there were any other option. You're the only hope {quest_stakes} have left.

playerResponses.text: Then I'll go deeper. Show me where this cave is.
Every time I come back you need more. This is the last time.
I've risked my neck enough already.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I hate to greet you with more bad news, but we've been hit again.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A second wave of {enemy_type} swept through while you were gone. They burned the storehouse where we were keeping the {quest_item} you already gathered. Everything is ash. {target_npc} managed to drag a few crates out the back, but it's barely a quarter of what we need to {quest_action}. The only option left is to scavenge from the old outpost {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know I keep asking more from you. I know. But {quest_stakes} have no one else.

playerResponses.text: Then I'll go to the outpost. We're not giving up.
Burned? Every time I turn around, things get worse. Fine, I'll go.
I've lost too much time on this already.

dialogueChunks.intro: Thank the stars you're still here, {player_name}. We need your help more urgently than ever.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} has spread to the very people trying to {quest_action}. {target_npc} is the only one still on their feet, and they're fading fast. Worse, a nest of {enemy_type} has moved into the caves where the last supply of {quest_item} grows. We need you to clear them out and gather what's left before we lose everything.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we lose the people doing the work, we lose {quest_stakes}. There's no softer way to say it.

playerResponses.text: I'll clear the caves and bring back everything I can find.
This was supposed to be a simple errand. Now it's a full campaign.
I'm not a healer or a miracle worker. I'm sorry.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... I don't know how to tell you this. Something has gone terribly wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The bridge you crossed to get the {quest_item} collapsed overnight: a landslide, triggered by the same rains that have been drowning us. The supply route to {quest_focus} is completely cut off. {target_npc} found an old game trail that winds {location_hint}, but it passes straight through {enemy_type} hunting grounds. We need you to scout that trail and make sure it's passable, or our efforts to {quest_action} are finished.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we can't reopen a supply line, everything you've done for {quest_stakes} will have been for nothing.

playerResponses.text: I'll scout the trail and clear whatever's in the way.
Nature and monsters both working against us. Just my luck.
A collapsed bridge changes everything. I can't keep risking my life like this.

dialogueChunks.intro: You won't believe what happened last night, {player_name}. I can barely believe it myself.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Wolves got into the livestock pen. Killed three goats and scattered the rest into the hills. Those animals were the only thing keeping {quest_stakes} fed while we worked to {quest_action}. {target_npc} tracked the herd {location_hint}, but the hills are thick with {enemy_type} and they couldn't go further alone. If we don't get those animals back and deliver the {quest_item}, two problems become a disaster.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're holding on by our fingernails here. One more setback and {quest_focus} won't matter anymore.

playerResponses.text: I'll track down the herd and finish what I started.
Livestock, monsters, supplies: this list never ends. Let's just get it done.
You've got too many problems for one person to solve.

dialogueChunks.intro: I was praying you'd come back soon, {player_name}. We're running out of time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A group of strangers arrived claiming their need for {quest_item} is greater than ours. {target_npc} tried to negotiate but they won't budge, and some of them are armed. We can't afford a fight and we can't afford to give up what we need to {quest_action}. The only solution is more: enough for everyone. There's a deposit {location_hint} that nobody's touched because of the {enemy_type} nesting nearby.
dialogueChunks.outro: If you can bring back enough for everyone, we avoid bloodshed. If not, {quest_stakes} are caught in the middle.

playerResponses.text: More for everyone is better than fighting over scraps. I'll go.
Sharing what I risked my life for? They'd better be grateful.
Your neighbors' problems are not mine. Sort it out yourselves.
